preflight.note: Created before implementation work starts for the ForgeLink-owned communications runtime, provider-neutral channels, contact metadata, telecom edge adapters, mobile companion design, and restored voice capability.

acceptance_criteria.text: Define the ForgeLink-owned local communications runtime for local inbox/outbox, message state, call state, approval state, channel registry, contact identity, contact policy, attention policy, retries, reconciliation, diagnostics, and operation without a telecom provider.

Define native channel, internet adapter, and telecom edge adapter contracts for capability discovery, local delivery, SMS/MMS send, voice call control, inbound webhook normalization, status update normalization, media normalization, provider errors, and credential validation.

Move existing Twilio SMS/MMS behavior behind the telecom edge adapter contract while preserving onboarding, webhook, status, media, retry, delivery behavior, and current user-visible messaging behavior.

Add local-only agent-to-human channel support so MCP/local API agent messages, approval requests, dismissals, and action records can be created, displayed, updated, and resolved through ForgeLink without SMS/MMS, voice, or third-party providers.

Design the ForgeLink mobile companion protocol with pairing, device identity, key exchange, local network transport, optional future relay boundary, redacted notifications, signed approve/deny responses, revocation, lost-device handling, and desktop as source of truth.
Add mobile companion planning gate with route/schema/stub contracts for future desktop-to-mobile pairing without shipping broken UI paths, unauthenticated routes, or public relay semantics.

Add Telnyx SMS/MMS telecom edge support with deterministic contract tests for sends, inbound SMS/MMS, delivery status, duplicate/backward events, invalid signatures, missing credentials, provider errors, and documentation.

Add Plivo and Bandwidth telecom edge planning gates or provider stubs with capability mapping, credential requirements, webhook validation requirements, MMS/media handling, and test fixture requirements without exposing unimplemented providers as shipped.

Add rich contact metadata including display name, avatar/media reference, notes, company, role/title, tags, favorite/pinned state, trust level, relationship/category, created/updated timestamps, and migration tests without data loss.

Add contact points and channel identities so contacts can have multiple labeled phone numbers, emails, native/mobile identities, and future channel identities; unknown inbound numbers can be attached, created, ignored, or blocked.
Add contact-level policy for agent messages, approval requests, urgent interrupts, quiet hours, mute/block behavior, and trust levels without giving unknown inbound contacts implicit privileges.

Define provider-neutral voice runtime and telecom edge contracts for local call state, outbound calls, inbound call events, call status, call end, caller/callee identity, call log persistence, provider errors, disabled states, and explicit telecom edge boundaries.

Implement Twilio Voice as the first voice telecom edge adapter through the TypeScript/Electron architecture with outbound call start/end, durable call rows, idempotent status callbacks, redacted errors, and automated tests.

Decide Twilio Verify scope and record whether phone verification is shipped, deferred as a provider capability, or out of scope; verification must not automatically grant trust without operator policy.

Add call UI surface with dialpad, selected contact, call/end controls, call status, unavailable/disabled states, provider/configuration hints, keyboard support, visual smoke, and renderer interaction tests.

Add durable contact-linked call history with direction, provider, from/to, contact resolution, status, timestamps, duration when available, provider ID, redacted error summary, retention/export/backup behavior, and diagnostics exclusion by default.

Add contact timeline merging SMS/MMS messages, future voice calls, local/mobile companion events, and agent-originated requests while preserving separation between ordinary communication and private agent approval details.

Add roadmap records for email, push, Telegram, WhatsApp, Discord, RSS, and related adapters, including intended use, privacy/security notes, likely credentials, inbound/outbound capability, quick-action support, failure modes, and Matrix exclusion rationale.

Add direct telecom research record covering SIP trunking, SMPP, SMSC/MMSC access, phone number provisioning, A2P/10DLC, STIR/SHAKEN, caller ID reputation, CNAM, E911/emergency scope, toll-free/short-code options, carrier partnership, costs, and why direct telecom is not the first implementation path.

Make first-run provider-optional: the app installs and onboards into a usable local-only state (agent approvals and the local human loop) with no telecom provider configured, and a 'Start local-only' path is offered so users are never forced to enter Twilio credentials; adding a provider is an explicit later step.

Add a shared provider conformance test kit: one reusable suite every SMS/MMS and voice edge adapter must pass (send success, send rejection, inbound message, duplicate inbound webhook, delivery status update, backward/duplicate status transition, invalid signature, missing credentials, media handling) so new providers meet one bar rather than ad hoc tests.

Establish a schema-migration coordination convention across the concurrently-active items 015/016/017 (sequential schema-version ownership and documented migration order) so their database changes do not collide, with each migration tested from the previously shipped schema.
